WASHINGTON, D. c., Dec. g.
The fight waged by Spokane and
other intermountain cities was
Drought to the attention of con-
gress yesterday by bill intro
duced by Senator Hepburn, of Ida
no. This bill is intended as an
amendment to the present law per-
hitting railroads to charge heavier
rates on inland hauls than for a
longer haul to coast terminals. At
present it is shown that railway
companies charge more for hauling
freight from Chicago to Spokane
than from Chicago to Seattle, and
other inland cities suffer by SANE
injustice.


The amendment has especial
reference to the claim that coast
terminals are entitled to discrim-
ination on account Of water trans
portatiOn competition, a claim
which is attacked as being I'M
worthy consideration in the case
of inter-mountain cities.
